query: Which database stores local metadata for Project Phoenix?

documents.text: Project Phoenix uses SQLite for local metadata and Qdrant for vector search.
Project Phoenix uses Qdrant for vector search over embedded memories.
Project Phoenix uses llama.cpp for endpoint smoke checks.
Project Phoenix can use Azure for cloud benchmark scale-out after quota is approved.

query: What is the current preferred local embedding model?

documents.text: The preferred local embedding model was old-embed-1024.
The current preferred local embedding model is nomic-embed-text because it is the working baseline.
The preferred local runtime for daily chat is Ollama.
BGE-M3 is a candidate embedder that needs side-by-side benchmarking before promotion.

query: Which benchmark uses distractor resistance cases?

documents.text: The Hermes tool-call benchmark uses strict held-out function-call cases.
The mem0 memory benchmark uses direct recall, recency conflict, and distractor resistance cases.
The runtime endpoint benchmark checks OpenAI-compatible model and chat endpoints.
The official benchmark environment smoke verifies packages such as lm-eval, BFCL, MTEB, and EvalPlus.

query: Where should Hermes export artifacts be stored?

documents.text: Hermes export artifacts should stay on /Volumes/PortableSSD/hermes-exports.
Source files, configs, benchmark fixtures, and reports should stay in Git.
Model caches should stay under /Volumes/PortableSSD/huggingface.
Benchmark outputs should stay under /Volumes/PortableSSD/hermes-evals.

query: Which extractor should remain the rollback extractor?

documents.text: The rollback extractor was hermes3:8b.
The rollback extractor is sam860/LFM2:2.6b until another extractor passes the benchmark gates.
The rollback embedder remains nomic-embed-text:latest.
Hermes 4 can be evaluated as a runtime baseline or teacher before local fine-tuning.

query: Which collection stores the current mem0 vectors?

documents.text: The active mem0 Qdrant collection is mem0_nomic_768.
The planned BGE-M3 candidate collection is mem0_bge_m3_1024.
The planned Qwen embedding candidate collection must verify dimensions before creation.
The LFM2-ColBERT candidate would use late interaction rather than the active mem0 vector collection.

query: What blocks publishing the Qwen3 v4 dataset?

documents.text: Publishing the Qwen3 v4 cleaned synthetic-only dataset is blocked pending explicit approval and re-audit.
Qwen3 v4 is the recommended strict Hermes tool-call adapter for local publication evidence.
Qwen3 4B GGUF exports exist for direct llama.cpp and LM Studio validation.
Azure GPU-family quota must be confirmed before submitting cloud benchmark jobs.

query: Which Qwen3 adapter is currently recommended for strict Hermes tool calls?

documents.text: Qwen3 v4 targeted is the current recommended strict Hermes tool-call adapter.
Qwen3 v5 pilot-polish is documented as a non-promotion because held-out strict pass regressed.
Qwen3 4B smoke training is a local MLX proof rather than the recommended public adapter.
Any Qwen3 v6 attempt must start from v4 and preserve held-out strict pass at 1.000.

query: What must be checked before creating Azure ML GPU compute?

documents.text: Azure ML GPU quota and regional capacity must be confirmed before compute creation.
The Azure for Students account login completed for d.a.mordaunt@gmail.com.
Mac MLX remains the local lane for achievable adapter training.
Official benchmark environments are installed and smoke-verified locally.

query: When should the Ollama Qwen3 runtime path be retested?

documents.text: Retest Ollama only after upgrading or replacing the current crashing Qwen3 GGUF import path.
LM Studio and direct llama.cpp are the validated GGUF runtime paths for the Qwen3 package.
The Qwen3 MLX adapter runtime smoke passed through an OpenAI-compatible mlx_lm.server endpoint.
Ollama model files are stored on /Volumes/PortableSSD/ollama-models.

query: What guard should a safer LFM2.5 recipe include?

documents.text: A safer LFM2.5 recipe should use a lower learning rate and an early empty-response gate.
The LFM2.5 full-smoke adapter is not publishable because evaluation showed response collapse.
LFM2-24B is a runtime baseline or teacher candidate, not a local fine-tuning default.
LFM2-ColBERT is a retrieval candidate that would require a late-interaction service proof.

query: Where should benchmark outputs be written to avoid internal disk pressure?

documents.text: Benchmark outputs should be written under /Volumes/PortableSSD/hermes-evals.
Small source files, configs, tests, and reports belong in the Git repository.
Hugging Face model caches should be kept under /Volumes/PortableSSD/huggingface.
Some bulky home cache directories can be relocated to /Volumes/PortableSSD/home-relocated.
